column6: Learner can greet people politely, introduce themselves at a basic level, ask who/what/where questions, and survive the first minute of a conversation.
column9: Learner can understand and say the digits needed for age, room numbers, and basic counting.
column10: Learner can recycle recent vocabulary while using number pronunciation control in short controlled output.
column13: ศูนย์ = zero; หนึ่ง = one; สอง = two; สาม = three; สี่ = four; ห้า = five; หก = six; เจ็ด = seven; แปด = eight; เก้า = nine
column14: สิบ = ten; เบอร์อะไร = what number?; ฉันอายุ...ปี = I am ... years old
column15: ประเทศ = country; ไทย = Thailand / Thai; ฉัน = I; ผม = I (male); คุณ = you; เขา = he / she
column16: Read Thai digits and associate them with spoken forms 0-10.
column17: Recognize slowly spoken digits in age and number contexts.
column18: Say your age, count objects, and give a simple number clearly.
column19: Digit recognition and production from zero to ten.
column22: Vocabulary system lesson. Widen usable lexicon without adding major new complexity.
column23: Thai With Fah first-contact pragmatics; ThaiPod101 introduction lessons; internal backwards planning.
